description: The 390 Duke is the bike that proved KTM could make a world-class naked streetfighter at an Indian price. 43 bhp, cornering ABS, quickshifter — it punches way above its weight class. If you want thrills without spending a fortune, this is the bike.
cons: Engine heat is uncomfortable in traffic
Seat gets uncomfortable after an hour
Wind blast at highway speeds is exhausting

common_problems.cause: High-performance liquid-cooled engine generates significant heat at low speeds
common_problems.solution: Park in shade, avoid prolonged idling. Engine cools quickly once moving.

common_problems.cause: USD forks are sensitive to dust and rough roads
common_problems.solution: Clean fork seals regularly. Replace fork oil every 15,000 km.

common_problems.cause: Track-focused seat design prioritises grip over comfort
common_problems.solution: Aftermarket gel seat or seat pad for tours. Stock is fine for short rides.

buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: OEM accessories for the 390 Duke offer perfect fitment and match the build quality. Aftermarket options are cheaper but check reviews carefully — some parts do not fit as well on premium bikes.
money_saving_tips: Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
Invest in quality riding gear — it protects you across every ride
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save money
Skipping crash guard — one tip-over can cost thousands in repairs
Using WD-40 as chain lube — it is a cleaner, not a lubricant
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
